rationale: The citation is used for more than background listing: the paper explicitly attributes the subset-conjunctive rule structure to Jedidi and Kohli (2005), including the claim that it generalizes conjunctive and disjunctive rules. That prior rule family is central to the citing paper's conceptual setup, because DOC is introduced as a broader rule class that nests subset-conjunctive forms. The citing study also evaluates subset-conjunctive models as benchmarks, so the cited work helps define the baseline being extended.
relation_description: The citing paper extends the subset-conjunctive line of work associated with Jedidi and Kohli (2005) by proposing DOC rules as a more general noncompensatory framework. It positions subset-conjunctive rules as nested special cases and as benchmark comparators in its empirical tests.
